summary: Phase 7 Pipeline (Kanban) frontend-only review. Verified login regression, sidebar/mobile nav 'Pipeline' between Leads & Follow-ups, /pipeline route accessible to both ADMIN & AGENT (no role guard), 6 columns with count badges, real Neon leads rendered, RBAC inherited (AGENT sees only own 2 cards, ADMIN sees 9), card content (name/source badge/phone/agent initials avatar/unassigned variant), drag-and-drop with optimistic UI + PUT /api/leads/:id (status only) returning 200 and counts shifting correctly (NEW 2→1, CONTACTED 3→4), URL state for search/agent/source filters with refresh persistence, clear-filters button resets URL, DragOverlay rendered, dropzone highlight class wiring correct, mobile (390x844) board has overflow-x-auto with fixed 280px columns, mobile drawer includes mobile-nav-pipeline in correct order. No regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: Drop-zone highlight relies on `isOver` from useDroppable — works, but the original spec mentioned ring-2 ring-primary/30 background tint always-on when a drag is in progress for any column. Current impl applies ring only to the column being hovered (isOver), and a lighter bg-muted/20 to other columns while dragging. This is a UX choice that may differ slightly from spec wording but is sensible. Confirm with product if all columns should glow during any drag.

PipelinePage.tsx fetches up to 500 leads at mount with no pagination/virtualisation. Fine for current Neon data (<10 leads) but consider virtualization or per-column lazy load once a tenant accumulates hundreds of leads.

In LeadCard.tsx the source badge label maps PROPERTY_PORTAL → 'Portal' (line 24). Spec said label should be 'Portal' (matches) — but the source filter dropdown shows 'Property Portal'. Slight inconsistency, but acceptable as the badge needs to be compact.
Optimistic rollback uses window.alert which is non-blocking but jarring. Consider sonner/toast for parity with rest of the app.

useDraggable on the lead.id means a card row that happens to have lead.id matching a status string would collide. Practically impossible (UUIDs vs enum strings) but worth noting; safer would be `card:${lead.id}` and `col:${status}` namespaces.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing Neon data (9 leads as ADMIN, 2 as AGENT).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pipeline page fully working. For drag-drop in Playwright, use mouse.move/down/up sequence with ~20 incremental moves and drop near TOP of pipeline-dropzone-{status} (avoid overlapping an existing card which can intercept). Wait at least 4-5s after mount before reading pipeline-count-{status} as the dual-fetch (leads + follow-ups) is slower than expected. PUT /api/leads/:id with body {status:'CONTACTED'} returns 200; on AGENT trying to update a non-owned lead, expect a 4xx and the optimistic update should roll back + window.alert (untested in this iteration since no cross-agent lead was available for the seeded agent).
verified_flows: Sidebar order: Dashboard > Leads > Pipeline > Follow-ups > Communications > Activity > ...
6 columns render with correct status labels and 'Negotiating' label for NEGOTIATING
ADMIN saw 9 leads (NEW:2, CONTACTED:3, QUALIFIED:2, NEGOTIATING:1, WON:1, LOST:0)
AGENT saw only 2 own leads (Demo Lead + Priya Sharma) in CONTACTED — RBAC inherited
Drag handle (GripVertical) drag → drop on dropzone fires PUT 200, counts update optimistically
Search input syncs ?search=, source filter syncs ?source=, clear-filters resets URL
Refresh with ?search=Priya&source=MANUAL restores both filter states
Mobile 390x844 board has overflow-x-auto, columns at 280px
Agent footer shows initials avatar + first name; Unassigned variant present
